_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Scalp avulsion resulting from hair entrapment in a rotating machine
Commonly occurring in Indian females working with industrial and agricultural machines.
Scalp usually separates where the scalp skin is thin and the cleavage line most commonly located in loose areolar and relatively less vascular tissue.
The periosteum is frequently found to be intact and rarely patient present with skull bone fracture.
Patients might present with partial or total loss of one or both ears along with the scalp.

Post procedure clinical photograph of the affected part

common_queries: Clinical notes/clinical photograph not indicative of need for scalp avulsion reconstruction
Clinical notes missing or incomplete for pre-authorization
Supporting reports or clinical photograph missing for pre-authorization
MLC/FIR report missing for traumatic cases
Detailed Indoor Case papers missing or incomplete for claim submission
Post-procedure clinical photograph of the affected part missing for claim submission

clinical_key_pointers: Proceed for Surgery only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical signs, symptoms, examination.
Scalp avulsion is a rare but severe injury and usually happens as an industrial accident.
Scalp avulsion resulting from hair entrapment in a rotating machine
Commonly occurring in Indian females working with industrial and agricultural machines.
In Indian settings when these patients coming late to the hospitals, where replantation is not possible and scalp reconstruction remains the only available option.
Reconstruction of large scalp defects present with some challenging problems.
Scalp avulsion injury leave distinctly a large area demanding large flap for cover.
Blood vessels are stretched and damaged because of avulsion injury; a flap with long pedicle is needed if we are to avoid the use of vein graft.
surgical_options: For Forehead Defects <50 cm²: Primary closure or local flap

For Forehead Defects <50 cm² (with Heavy trauma, osteomyelitis or osteoradionecrosis, previous radiation or radiation ulcer, previous local flap failure, and radical postoperative radiation): Scapular free flap
For Forehead Defects >50 cm²: Scapular free flap
For Forehead Defects >50 cm² (with a Scalp defect): Scapular free flap or latissimus free flap with STSG
For Scalp Defects <200 cm²: Primary closure or local flap

For Scalp Defects <200 cm² (with Heavy trauma, osteomyelitis or osteoradionecrosis, previous radiation or radiation ulcer, previous local flap failure, and radical postoperative radiation): Rectus abdominis or latissimus dorsi free flap (muscles only) with STSG
For Scalp Defects 200-600 cm²: Latissimus dorsi muscle free flap with STSG
For Scalp Defects >600 cm²: Two latissimus dorsi muscle free flaps with STSG
